behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-handler handoff, not just JSON.parse in isolation. MOCK-MISS: this behavior includes 'proceed through normal hook input handling', so a pure parser unit test is too narrow. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP are disqualified by behavior text: no real OS/network/subprocess, no AI model output, no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Wire hook input handler modules in-process (payload source -> parser -> normal handler path) with a valid JSON payload whose string fields include code fences, backticks, and $vars. Assert parse succeeds and downstream handler receives expected structured data/state.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: Need parser + trace-record construction/sink wiring to verify diagnostic context content, so this is beyond one local function. Higher levels rejected by behavior text: it says malformed JSON and trace record content, not real process/OS behavior or model-dependent judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process malformed payload through hook input pipeline with a test trace sink. Assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes diagnostic fields (error location/snippet/offset or equivalent) sufficient to identify malformed section.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is specifically policy mapping plus telemetry intent flagging after parse failure, which is module interaction (error policy + telemetry). System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: 'local error-policy path' indicates no required real infra and no AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Inject parse-failure outcome into local error-policy flow with telemetry collector stub. Assert function returns handled/non-throw result and telemetry intent marker is set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA is explicit: 'real process execution' and malformed stdin/exit behavior require subprocess + OS I/O semantics. Mocked in-process tests can hide crash/exit ordering failures. Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text: no model call or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook process, write malformed bytes/JSON to stdin, close stdin, and wait for exit. Assert non-crash behavior (expected controlled exit) and confirm expected trace signal is emitted before process termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires the real invocation path including stdin boundary and trace artifact emission; that is an end-to-end process/IO boundary. Integration rejected because behavior explicitly says 'real hook invocation path'. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Execute hook via its real invocation entrypoint, pass malformed input through stdin, and verify produced trace artifact at the real sink location/format contains the expected parse-failure signal.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: This is a comparative regression across two input classes through shared hook-input handling and failure attribution logic; interaction-level behavior is the target. System rejected because behavior text does not require real process/OS boundary. Agentic/Workflow rejected: deterministic parsing/attribution, no AI output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterized in-process regression suite with (a) valid markdown-heavy JSON payloads and (b) malformed JSON payloads. Assert only malformed cases emit parse-failure trace/error path, while valid markdown cases follow normal handling with no parse-failure attribution.
